comment: Advisories that are ACCEPTED for now, checked by scripts/audit-allowlist.mjs.
An entry belongs here only when the fix is genuinely not ours to make. If a version
bump or an npm `overrides` entry can resolve it, do that instead — see shell-quote
in the root package.json for an example of the latter.
Every entry expires. On the expiry date CI fails and the exception has to be
re-argued: re-check upstream, then either fix it or extend the date with a fresh
reason. An entry that stops matching also fails, so a fixed advisory cannot sit here
pretending to be a live risk.
Fields: id (GHSA), package, directory (which audited tree it applies to), severity,
reason, blocker (why we cannot fix it), expires (YYYY-MM-DD), review (what to check).

allow.reason: DoS via exponential-time expansion of consecutive non-expanding {} groups. Reached only through @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent's own minimatch, expanding glob patterns we author ourselves — not attacker-supplied input.
allow.blocker: pi-coding-agent 0.80.10 ships an npm-shrinkwrap.json pinning brace-expansion 5.0.6, and a dependency's shrinkwrap wins over a consumer's overrides, so neither `overrides` nor `npm dedupe` nor `npm audit fix` can move it. 0.80.10 is the latest published version, so there is nothing newer to upgrade to. The root's own copy is already patched at 5.0.7.
allow.review: Check for a pi-coding-agent release above 0.80.10 whose shrinkwrap carries brace-expansion >= 5.0.7.

allow.reason: DoS via infinite loop when parsing .proto options. Reached only through pi-coding-agent's nested @google/genai; we do not parse untrusted .proto files.
allow.blocker: Same pinned shrinkwrap in pi-coding-agent 0.80.10, which fixes protobufjs at 7.6.4. The root's own copy is already patched at 7.6.5.
allow.review: Check for a pi-coding-agent release above 0.80.10 whose shrinkwrap carries protobufjs >= 7.6.5.
